note: Mandatory seat reservations for DiscoverEU / Interrail pass holders in 2026. Prices are typical, not live — always verify with the operator before booking. A 'mandatory' entry means the pass is not valid on that leg without an extra paid reservation.

reservations.notes: Every train across the French–Italian border requires a reservation. Book 2+ weeks ahead for €10–35; last-minute can exceed €50.
Paris–Barcelona high-speed trains need reservations — limited pass-holder quota, book early.
TGV trains crossing into Germany need reservations. ICE on the same corridor is reservation-free but slower.
TGV Lyria is the only direct high-speed option; reservations sell out weeks ahead in summer.
All Italian high-speed trains require a reservation (€13 with the pass). Regional trains do not.
Spanish AVE high-speed requires paid reservations (€10 for pass holders). Cercanías and media distancia are free.
Nightjet sleeper reservations start at €29 (seat), €59 (couchette), €99+ (private sleeper).
International trains across the Croatia–Hungary border require a reservation; seats are limited.
All Swedish SJ night trains require a reservation (€25 seat, more for berths).
Cross-border trains between Bulgaria and Greece require a reservation and run infrequently — plan ahead.
The Corona / Dacia daily trains between Bucharest and Budapest require a reservation.
Turkish bonus: the overnight Sofia Express is the only direct rail link between Istanbul and the DiscoverEU network. Sleeper reservation required.
